--- SOURCE 1: Safety and tolerance of the ReWalk™ exoskeleton suit for ... ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3304563/

SUMMARY:
There were no adverse safety events. Use of the system was generally well tolerated, with no increase in pain and a moderate level of fatigue after use . Individuals with lower level of spinal cord injury performed walking more efficiently.

enriched_points.expanded_content: ReWalk™ is a robotic exoskeleton enabling individuals with lower limb impairments to walk, demonstrating safety, tolerance, and improved walking efficiency in clinical trials.
Ekso™ is an advanced exoskeleton designed for rehabilitation, allowing users to walk and regain mobility, with demonstrated effectiveness in improving gait mechanics and reducing fatigue.
Other notable models include versatile designs focusing on various rehabilitation needs, showcasing innovative technologies aimed at enhancing mobility and independence for users.
Clinical trials for these exoskeletons report positive outcomes, including improved gait performance and user safety, reinforcing their potential in rehabilitation settings.
